content.text:
```json
{
  "signature": "This four-voice fugue in A minor navigates a wide arc of related keys before contracting back to its home — moving through C major (bar 9), E minor (bar 21), D minor (bar 36), and a bracing stretch of E major (bars 56–60) before the most arresting moment in the piece: bar 70's modal-mixture shift to F major, approached via the flat-sixth chord (bVI), which pulls the harmony briefly onto the flat side of the scale just as the voices begin pressing together in stretto. That stretto section (bars 70–79) also deploys a bVII and a striking bII chord (bar 74) before the final cadence resolves with a perfect authentic cadence at bar 87. The result is a large-scale structure shaped like a journey outward and back — diatonic modulations throughout, but with one moment of modal darkness near the end that makes the homecoming feel earned.",
  "significance": "That sudden move to F major in bar 70, coming after the brightness of E major, creates a sense of the ground shifting beneath you — like a late cloud crossing the sun — and the voices crowding together in imitation gives the ending an urgency and inevitability that the earlier, more spacious sections deliberately withhold. The final A minor cadence lands with all the more weight because the harmony has just wandered somewhere unexpected.",
  "connection": "The immediately adjacent Fugue 19 in A major (BWV 864b) shares this fugue's preference for clean, diatonic modulations and a similar cadential vocabulary of perfect authentic and half cadences placed at carefully spaced structural pillars — habits that reflect Bach's larger plan for the Well-Tempered Clavier as a compendium of contrasting but related approaches within a single key area. Where Fugue 20 finds its expressive centre of gravity through modal mixture and a flat-side detour near the close, Fugue 19 more typically stays within the brighter diatonic world its major key affords, suggesting Bach consciously paired the two as mirror images: one tilting toward light, the other toward shadow. Together they demonstrate how the same contrapuntal architecture can carry entirely different emotional weather simply by where the harmony chooses to lean in the final stretch."
}
```
